title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
code: # Document (regenerate NAMESPACE + man/) devtools::document()  # Run all tests devtools::test()  # Run a single test file devtools::test(filter = "dt_stations")  # Full R CMD check devtools::check()  # Install the package locally devtools::install()  # Rebuild the bundled station cache (R/sysdata.rda) — run before a release source("data-raw/stations_detailed.R")

title: CLAUDE.md
text: public functions follow pattern: Validate inputs (check_id(), check_date(), check_bbox() R/utils.R) Call dt_get_json() dt_get_csv() (R/client.R) Pass response parse_*() function (R/parsers.R) Return tidy tibble

title: CLAUDE.md
text: dt_base_request() builds httr2 request shared User-Agent, 30-second timeout, retry logic (5× 429/500/503), 10 req/min throttle. dt_perform() wraps req_perform() converts HTTP errors classed cli_abort() conditions (digitraffic_error_not_found, digitraffic_error_rate_limit, digitraffic_error_network, digitraffic_error_http). functions call either dt_get_json() dt_get_csv(), compose request return parsed body.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Two independent caching layers: Session cache (.dt_cache environment): dt_cache_set()/dt_cache_get() 5-minute TTL. Used dt_stations() dt_sensors() avoid redundant API calls within session. Detailed station cache: Stores municipality/province/road metadata ~490 stations. Priority order: (1) user disk cache tools::R_user_dir("digitraffic", "cache")/stations_detailed.rds, (2) bundled snapshot R/sysdata.rda (object stations_detailed_cache, regenerated data-raw/stations_detailed.R). dt_stations_load_details() fetches station details parallel via httr2::req_perform_parallel() saves disk cache.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Two distinct IDs exist per station — interchangeable: id: internal API identifier, used dt_station() dt_station_data() (JSON endpoints) tms_number: TMS system number, used dt_history_raw() (CSV endpoint URL path)

title: CLAUDE.md
text: JSON → tibble CSV → tibble conversions internal. GeoJSON FeatureCollections flattened parse_stations_response(). %||% null-coalescing operator (R/utils.R) used throughout handle missing API fields gracefully. Historical CSV files header — column names types defined .lam_csv_col_names / .lam_csv_col_types parsed readr::read_delim().

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Tests use httptest2::with_mock_dir("fixtures", {...}) replay recorded API responses stored tests/testthat/fixtures/tie.digitraffic.fi/. Tests need assert specific HTTP error classes use httr2::with_mocked_responses() directly. helper.R file loads httptest2 test files.

title: Getting started with digitraffic
text: Digitraffic open API maintained Finntraffic provides real-time historical traffic data Finland’s road network. data comes 450 LAM (Liikenteen Automaattinen Mittaus / Automatic Traffic Measurement) stations — electromagnetic induction loops embedded road surface detect every passing vehicle. LAM station records: Speed (km/h) Vehicle length (metres) — used classification Direction lane Headway — time gap previous vehicle Vehicle classification — 7 categories cars HCT trucks digitraffic R package wraps API endpoints returns data tidy tibbles, ready analysis dplyr visualisation ggplot2.

title: Getting started with digitraffic
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("bbtheo/digitraffic")

title: Getting started with digitraffic
code: library(digitraffic) library(dplyr)

title: Getting started with digitraffic
text: first thing ’ll want find stations interest. dt_stations() returns tibble LAM stations coordinates, bearing, operational status: result cached 5 minutes repeated calls instant. dt_stations() supports five filter parameters — see vignette("station-filtering") full guide. Quick examples:
code: stations <- dt_stations() stations # By name (case-insensitive regex) dt_stations(name = "Espoo")  # By road number (parsed from name, no extra API calls) dt_stations(road_number = 1)  # By geographic bounding box dt_stations(bbox = c(24.5, 60.1, 25.2, 60.4))  # By municipality or province (uses bundled metadata cache) dt_stations(municipality = "Espoo") dt_stations(province = "Uusimaa")  # Filters combine — road 4 stations in Pirkanmaa dt_stations(road_number = 4, province = "Pirkanmaa")

title: Getting started with digitraffic
text: single station, dt_station() returns extended set 32 fields including municipality, province, road number, road section, free-flow speeds, multilingual names:
code: detail <- dt_station(23001) detail  # Key fields detail |>   select(name, municipality, province, road_number,          free_flow_speed1, free_flow_speed2)

title: Getting started with digitraffic
text: sf package installed, can convert stations tibble spatial object mapping:
code: library(sf)  stations_sf <- dt_stations() |>   st_as_sf(coords = c("longitude", "latitude"), crs = 4326)  # Filter to active stations only active <- stations_sf |>   filter(collection_status == "GATHERING")  plot(active["bearing"], pch = 20, main = "LAM stations coloured by bearing")

title: Getting started with digitraffic
text: dt_station_data() fetches current sensor readings one station. Data updated approximately every minute: row single sensor measurement. name column contains structured Finnish name encoding measurement type, interval, direction. example:
code: data <- dt_station_data(23001) data

title: Getting started with digitraffic
code: data |>   filter(unit == "km/h") |>   select(name, value, unit, time_window_start, time_window_end)

title: Getting started with digitraffic
code: data |>   filter(unit == "kpl/h") |>   select(name, value, unit, time_window_start, time_window_end)

title: Getting started with digitraffic
text: dt_stations_data() fetches sensor data every station one request. response several MB, takes seconds:
code: all_data <- dt_stations_data()  # How many stations have data? n_distinct(all_data$station_id)  # Average 60-min speed across all stations, direction 1 all_data |>   filter(name == "KESKINOPEUS_60MIN_KIINTEA_SUUNTA1") |>   summarise(     n_stations    = n(),     mean_speed    = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),     median_speed  = median(value, na.rm = TRUE),     min_speed     = min(value, na.rm = TRUE),     max_speed     = max(value, na.rm = TRUE)   )

title: Getting started with digitraffic
text: 76 different sensor types. dt_sensors() returns full catalogue descriptions: can join catalogue sensor data enrich output:
code: sensors <- dt_sensors() sensors  # Speed sensors sensors |> filter(unit == "km/h")  # Volume sensors sensors |> filter(unit == "kpl/h")  # Status / special sensors sensors |> filter(unit == "***") dt_station_data(23001) |>   left_join(     sensors |> select(sensor_id = id, description_fi, direction),     by = "sensor_id"   )

title: Getting started with digitraffic
text: station calibration constants define reference values specific road segment: constants include: Many stations seasonal variants different summer (Apr-Oct) winter (Nov-Mar) values:
code: # All constants consts <- dt_sensor_constants() consts  # Constants for one station dt_sensor_constants(id = 23001) dt_sensor_constants(id = 20002) |>   filter(grepl("VVAPAAS", name)) |>   select(name, value, valid_from, valid_to) #> # A tibble: 4 x 4 #>   name     value valid_from valid_to #>   <chr>    <dbl> <chr>      <chr> #> 1 VVAPAAS1   105 04-01      10-31 #> 2 VVAPAAS1   100 11-01      03-31 #> 3 VVAPAAS2   105 04-01      10-31 #> 4 VVAPAAS2   100 11-01      03-31

title: Getting started with digitraffic
text: filtering stations road, municipality, province, bounding box, see vignette("station-filtering") historical per-vehicle data, see vignette("historical-data") full function reference, see package documentation site

title: Working with historical raw data
text: Digitraffic API stores raw vehicle-level observations daily CSV files every LAM station. row represents single vehicle passing measurement loop, capturing speed, length, lane, direction, vehicle class — centisecond. dt_history_raw() function downloads parses files tidy tibbles, enriched proper datetime column human-readable vehicle class labels. Coverage: Available December 2021 onwards Published following day 08:00-09:00 EET Typically 10,000-50,000 rows per station per day (busy highways)

title: Working with historical raw data
code: library(digitraffic) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2)

title: Working with historical raw data
text: fetching history, need station’s tms_number (id!). two identifiers different: id — used dt_station_data() dt_station() real-time data tms_number — used dt_history_raw() historical CSV files
code: # Find stations in Helsinki area dt_stations(name = "Helsinki") |>   select(id, tms_number, name) #> # A tibble: 12 x 3 #>       id tms_number name #>    <int>      <int> <chr> #>  1 23101        101 vt4_Helsinki_Tattarisuo #>  2 23102        102 vt3_Helsinki_Haaga #>  …

title: Working with historical raw data
text: result 17 columns:
code: raw <- dt_history_raw(tms_number = 1, date = as.Date("2024-06-15")) raw

title: Working with historical raw data
text: vehicle_class column uses integer codes 1-9. full mapping available via dt_vehicle_classes(): vehicle_class_label column dt_history_raw() output already joins labels .
code: dt_vehicle_classes() #> # A tibble: 9 x 5 #>   vehicle_class label_en                   label_fi  category_en category_fi #>           <int> <chr>                      <chr>     <chr>       <chr> #> 1             1 Car / van                  ... #> 2             2 Truck (no trailer)         ... #> 3             3 Bus                        ... #> 4             4 Truck + semitrailer        ... #> 5             5 Truck + full trailer       ... #> 6             6 Car + trailer              ... #> 7             7 Car + caravan / motorhome  ... #> 8             8 Motorcycle / moped         ... #> 9             9 High Capacity Truck        ...

title: Working with historical raw data
text: Always filter quality_flag == 0 analysis. flag 1 indicates observation fell outside valid measurement ranges: Speed: 2-199 km/h Vehicle length: 1-39.8 m Hour: 0-23 Direction: 1-2 Vehicle class: 1-9 Lane: >= 1
code: # Remove faulty observations clean <- raw |>   filter(quality_flag == 0)  # Check how many were removed nrow(raw) - nrow(clean)

title: Working with historical raw data
code: hourly <- raw |>   filter(quality_flag == 0) |>   count(hour, direction, name = "vehicles")  ggplot(hourly, aes(x = hour, y = vehicles, colour = factor(direction))) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:23) +   labs(     title = "Hourly traffic volume",     x = "Hour of day",     y = "Number of vehicles",     colour = "Direction"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Working with historical raw data
code: raw |>   filter(quality_flag == 0, vehicle_class == 1) |>   ggplot(aes(x = speed_kmh)) +   geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, fill = "steelblue", colour = "white") +   facet_wrap(~direction, labeller = labeller(direction = c(     "1" = "Direction 1", "2" = "Direction 2"   ))) +   labs(     title = "Speed distribution (cars/vans only)",     x = "Speed (km/h)",     y = "Count"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Working with historical raw data
code: raw |>   filter(quality_flag == 0) |>   count(vehicle_class_label, sort = TRUE) |>   mutate(pct = round(n / sum(n) * 100, 1)) #> # A tibble: 9 x 3 #>   vehicle_class_label          n   pct #>   <chr>                    <int> <dbl> #> 1 Car / van                24891  87.3 #> 2 Truck + full trailer      1532   5.4 #> 3 Truck + semitrailer        982   3.4 #> …

what: Average speed by hour and vehicle type
title: Working with historical raw data
code: raw |>   filter(quality_flag == 0) |>   mutate(type = if_else(vehicle_class_category == "Car", "Car", "Heavy")) |>   group_by(hour, type) |>   summarise(mean_speed = mean(speed_kmh), .groups = "drop") |>   ggplot(aes(x = hour, y = mean_speed, colour = type)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:23) +   labs(     title = "Average speed by hour: cars vs heavy vehicles",     x = "Hour of day",     y = "Mean speed (km/h)",     colour = "Vehicle type"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Working with historical raw data
text: interval_ms column records time gap consecutive vehicles lane. useful studying congestion following distances:
code: raw |>   filter(quality_flag == 0, interval_ms > 0, interval_ms < 60000) |>   ggplot(aes(x = interval_ms / 1000)) +   geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, fill = "darkorange", colour = "white") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 30)) +   labs(     title = "Headway distribution (time gap to previous vehicle)",     x = "Headway (seconds)",     y = "Count"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Working with historical raw data
text: analyse multiple days, loop dates lapply() purrr::map():
code: dates <- seq(as.Date("2024-06-10"), as.Date("2024-06-14"), by = "day")  week_data <- lapply(dates, function(d) {   dt_history_raw(tms_number = 1, date = d) |>     mutate(date = d) }) |>   bind_rows()  # Daily volume comparison week_data |>   filter(quality_flag == 0) |>   count(date, name = "daily_total") |>   mutate(weekday = format(date, "%A"))

title: Working with historical raw data
text: day’s CSV file separate API request, typically 1-5 MB. large date ranges: Consider spacing requests stay 60 req/min throttle Store intermediate results locally saveRDS() / readRDS() bulk analysis (months years), consider using pre-computed statistical reports https://tie.digitraffic.fi/ui/tms/history/

title: Working with historical raw data
text: Combine real-time historical data sensor constants detect congestion — .e., observed speeds drop significantly free-flow reference:
code: # Get the free-flow speed for direction 1 consts <- dt_sensor_constants(id = 23001) freeflow <- consts |>   filter(name == "VVAPAAS1") |>   pull(value)  # Compute hourly mean speed and compare to free flow raw |>   filter(quality_flag == 0, direction == 1) |>   group_by(hour) |>   summarise(mean_speed = mean(speed_kmh)) |>   mutate(     freeflow_speed = freeflow,     speed_pct = round(mean_speed / freeflow_speed * 100, 1),     congested = speed_pct < 70   )

title: Working with historical raw data
text: common workflow look station details, fetch historical data:
code:
# 1. Find a station station <- dt_stations(name = "Espoo") |>   slice(1)  # 2. Get its detailed metadata detail <- dt_station(station$id) cat("Station:", detail$name, "
") cat("Road:", detail$road_number, "
") cat("Municipality:", detail$municipality, "
") cat("Province:", detail$province, "
")  # 3. Fetch yesterday's raw data using tms_number raw <- dt_history_raw(   tms_number = station$tms_number,   date = Sys.Date() - 1 )  # 4. Summarise raw |>   filter(quality_flag == 0) |>   summarise(     total_vehicles = n(),     mean_speed     = round(mean(speed_kmh), 1),     pct_heavy      = round(mean(vehicle_class >= 2) * 100, 1)   )

title: Working with historical raw data
text: Digitraffic road traffic documentation (Finnish/English) Digitraffic LAM documentation — detailed sensor descriptions API Swagger documentation — full endpoint reference Getting started vignette — real-time data, sensors, station metadata Station filtering vignette — filtering road, municipality, province, bounding box

title: Filtering and discovering stations
text: Finland’s LAM network 500 measurement stations spread across road network. dt_stations() supports five complementary filter parameters can narrow exactly stations need without writing manual dplyr::filter() calls. filters combinable applied order listed .
code: library(digitraffic) library(dplyr)

title: Filtering and discovering stations
text: simplest filter — case-insensitive regular expression matched station name string. Station names encode road type, road number, location, e.g. "vt1_Espoo_Hirvisuo":
code: # Stations whose name contains "Espoo" (case-insensitive) dt_stations(name = "Espoo")  # Exact road prefix — valtatie 1 only dt_stations(name = "^vt1_")  # All kantatie stations dt_stations(name = "^kt")  # Helsinki or Espoo in the name dt_stations(name = "Helsinki|Espoo")

title: Filtering and discovering stations
text: road_number extracts integer road number station name string — extra API calls required. naming convention covers Finnish road types: Stations whose names don’t follow standard convention return NA parsed road number silently excluded road_number filter results.
code: # All stations on valtatie 7 (Helsinki–Russian border) dt_stations(road_number = 7)  # All stations on the Ring Road III (kt50) dt_stations(road_number = 50)  # Stations on roads 1 through 5 lapply(1:5, \(rn) dt_stations(road_number = rn)) |>   dplyr::bind_rows()

title: Filtering and discovering stations
text: Pass length-4 numeric vector c(lon_min, lat_min, lon_max, lat_max) WGS-84 degrees. Works bulk coordinates already returned dt_stations() — extra API calls: can combine bbox road_number find stations specific road within geographic area:
code: # Helsinki metropolitan area dt_stations(bbox = c(24.5, 60.1, 25.2, 60.4))  # Greater Tampere region dt_stations(bbox = c(23.4, 61.3, 24.1, 61.7))  # Entire southern Finland dt_stations(bbox = c(22.0, 59.8, 27.0, 61.5)) # valtatie 4 stations south of Lahti dt_stations(road_number = 4, bbox = c(25.5, 60.9, 26.5, 61.2))

title: Filtering and discovering stations
text: sf package, can compute bounding box polygon:
code: library(sf)  # Example: use a municipality polygon to build a bbox # espoo_polygon <- ... (loaded from a shapefile or administrative boundary API) # bb <- st_bbox(espoo_polygon) # dt_stations(bbox = c(bb["xmin"], bb["ymin"], bb["xmax"], bb["ymax"]))

title: Filtering and discovering stations
text: filters use detailed station metadata — 32 fields per station including municipality, province, road address, multilingual names. data served cache API calls made filter time. Combine filters freely:
code: # All stations in Espoo dt_stations(municipality = "Espoo")  # Stations in Tampere (case-insensitive) dt_stations(municipality = "tampere")  # All stations in Helsinki or Espoo (regex) dt_stations(municipality = "Helsinki|Espoo") # All stations in Pirkanmaa province dt_stations(province = "Pirkanmaa")  # Stations in either of two provinces dt_stations(province = "Uusimaa|Pirkanmaa") # valtatie 3 stations in Pirkanmaa dt_stations(road_number = 3, province = "Pirkanmaa")  # Stations in Espoo within a tight bounding box dt_stations(municipality = "Espoo", bbox = c(24.5, 60.1, 25.0, 60.4))

title: Filtering and discovering stations
text: Municipality province filtering relies detailed metadata cache holds 32 fields per station (vs. 10 fields bulk endpoint).

title: Filtering and discovering stations
text: cache built fetching individual station endpoint (/api/tms/v1/stations/{id}) every station. includes: municipality / municipality_code province / province_code road_number / road_section / distance_from_section_start direction1_municipality / direction2_municipality free_flow_speed1 / free_flow_speed2 name_fi / name_sv / name_en — see ?dt_station full list

title: Filtering and discovering stations
text: bundled snapshot always present — dt_stations(municipality = "Espoo") works immediately installing package, setup required.
code: Priority 1 (user disk):  tools::R_user_dir("digitraffic", "cache")/stations_detailed.rds                                   |                          (falls back if not present)                                   | Priority 2 (bundled):    R/sysdata.rda  (baked into the package at build time)

title: Filtering and discovering stations
text: time use detail-based filter, package compares cached station IDs current live station list dt_stations(). differ (new stations added, old ones decommissioned), ’ll see: filter still runs — just uses slightly stale cache.
code: Warning: Detailed station cache is out of date: 3 new stations detected. i Run `dt_stations_load_details()` to refresh.

title: Filtering and discovering stations
text: network changed, run: uses httr2::req_perform_parallel() 10 concurrent connections. typical connection completes 15 seconds. result saved personal cache directory persists across R sessions. control concurrency:
code: dt_stations_load_details() #> Fetching detailed metadata for 521 stations... #> [working] ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■  100% | ETA:  0s #> v Saved detailed metadata for 521 stations. #> i Cache location: ~/.cache/R/digitraffic/stations_detailed.rds # More aggressive (faster on a fast connection) dt_stations_load_details(max_active = 20)  # More conservative (politer to the API) dt_stations_load_details(max_active = 5)

what: Using filtered results with sensor data
title: Filtering and discovering stations
text: common pattern: filter stations first, fetch sensor data :
code: # Get all stations in Uusimaa uusimaa <- dt_stations(province = "Uusimaa")  # Fetch real-time data for the first 5 sensor_data <- lapply(head(uusimaa$id, 5), dt_station_data) |>   dplyr::bind_rows()  # Average speed in each direction sensor_data |>   filter(grepl("KESKINOPEUS_60MIN.*SUUNTA", name)) |>   group_by(name) |>   summarise(mean_speed = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE))

title: Filtering and discovering stations
text: Combine filtering sf spatial analysis mapping:
code: library(sf) library(ggplot2)  # Stations in Pirkanmaa projected to Finnish TM35FIN (EPSG 3067) pirkanmaa_sf <- dt_stations(province = "Pirkanmaa") |>   st_as_sf(coords = c("longitude", "latitude"), crs = 4326) |>   st_transform(3067)  # Plot with ggplot2 ggplot(pirkanmaa_sf) +   geom_sf(aes(colour = collection_status), size = 2) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c(     "GATHERING"           = "#2166ac",     "REMOVED_TEMPORARILY" = "#d73027"   )) +   labs(     title  = "LAM stations in Pirkanmaa",     colour = "Status"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Filtering and discovering stations
code: # Quick reference — all these are equivalent to chaining dplyr::filter() calls # but done in a single function with no repeated API calls  dt_stations()                                    # all 500+ stations dt_stations(name = "^vt1_")                      # name regex dt_stations(road_number = 1)                     # road number from name dt_stations(bbox = c(24.5, 60.1, 25.2, 60.4))   # bounding box dt_stations(municipality = "Espoo")              # municipality (cache) dt_stations(province = "Uusimaa")                # province (cache)  # Combined — all filters applied in sequence dt_stations(   road_number  = 1,   province     = "Uusimaa",   bbox         = c(24.5, 60.1, 25.2, 60.4) )

title: Filtering and discovering stations
text: dt_station() — detailed metadata single station (32 fields) dt_stations_load_details() — refresh detailed metadata cache vignette("digitraffic") — getting started real-time sensor data vignette("historical-data") — historical per-vehicle analysis

what: R Client for the Finntraffic Digitraffic API
title: R Client for the Finntraffic Digitraffic API
text: digitraffic R client Finntraffic Digitraffic open API, giving tidy access Finland’s automatic traffic measurement (LAM/TMS) network — 450 road stations measuring vehicle speeds, volumes, classifications real time. data returned tibbles snake_case column names, ready analysis dplyr, ggplot2, rest tidyverse.

title: R Client for the Finntraffic Digitraffic API
text: Install development version GitHub:
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("bbtheo/digitraffic")

title: R Client for the Finntraffic Digitraffic API
code: library(digitraffic)  # List all measurement stations — road_number, municipality, province always included dt_stations() #> # A tibble: 489 x 13 #>       id tms_number name               road_number longitude latitude elevation #>    <int>      <int> <chr>                    <int>     <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl> #>  1 20002      20002 vt1_Espoo_Hirvisuo           1      24.6     60.2        30 #>  2 23001          1 vt7_Rita                     7      25.7     60.4         0 #>  # … with 6 more variables: bearing <int>, municipality <chr>, province <chr>, #>  #   collection_status <chr>, state <chr>, data_updated_time <dttm>  # Search by name (supports regex) dt_stations(name = "Espoo")  # Detailed metadata for one station dt_station(23001) #> # A tibble: 1 x 32 #>      id tms_number name     municipality province road_number free_flow_speed1 #>   <int>      <int> <chr>    <chr>        <chr>          <int>            <dbl> #> 1 23001          1 vt7_Rita Porvoo       Uusimaa            7              105  # Real-time speed and volume data dt_station_data(23001) #> # A tibble: 40 x 11 #>    station_id sensor_id name                            value unit  measured_time #>         <int>     <int> <chr>                           <dbl> <chr> <dttm> #>  1      23001      5016 OHITUKSET_5MIN_KIINTEA_SUUNTA1   216 kpl/h 2024-06-01 12:00:00 #>  2      23001      5057 KESKINOPEUS_60MIN_KIINTEA_SU…    107 km/h  2024-06-01 12:00:00 #>  …  # Historical per-vehicle data (one station, one day) raw <- dt_history_raw(tms_number = 1, date = as.Date("2024-03-15")) #> # A tibble: 28,472 x 17 #>    station_id speed_kmh vehicle_class_label datetime #>         <int>     <int> <chr>               <dttm> #>  1          1        97 Car / van           2024-03-15 08:00:12 #>  2          1        84 Truck (no trailer)  2024-03-15 08:00:15 #>  …

title: R Client for the Finntraffic Digitraffic API
code: # By road number (no extra API calls) dt_stations(road_number = 7)  # By municipality or province (uses bundled metadata cache) dt_stations(municipality = "Espoo") dt_stations(province = "Uusimaa")  # By bounding box dt_stations(bbox = c(24.5, 60.1, 25.2, 60.4))  # Combine freely dt_stations(road_number = 4, province = "Pirkanmaa")  # Refresh the metadata cache when new stations are added dt_stations_load_details()

title: R Client for the Finntraffic Digitraffic API
code: library(dplyr)  dt_history_raw(tms_number = 1, date = as.Date("2024-06-15")) |>   filter(quality_flag == 0, direction == 1) |>   count(hour, name = "vehicles") |>   print(n = 24)

title: R Client for the Finntraffic Digitraffic API
code: dt_stations_data() |>   filter(grepl("KESKINOPEUS_60MIN.*SUUNTA1", name)) |>   summarise(mean_speed_kmh = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE))

title: R Client for the Finntraffic Digitraffic API
code: dt_sensor_constants() |>   filter(name == "VVAPAAS1") |>   arrange(desc(value)) |>   head(20)

what: Convert stations to sf for mapping
title: R Client for the Finntraffic Digitraffic API
code: library(sf) stations <- dt_stations() |>   st_as_sf(coords = c("longitude", "latitude"), crs = 4326)

title: R Client for the Finntraffic Digitraffic API
text: Base URL: https://tie.digitraffic.fi Authentication: None required (open data) Rate limiting: package throttles 60 requests/minute (Digitraffic’s default limit) retries transient errors (429, 500, 503) automatically Caching: Station metadata cached 5 minutes per R session; call dt_cache_clear() refresh Historical data: Available December 2021 onwards; published next day 08:00–09:00 EET

title: R Client for the Finntraffic Digitraffic API
text: data provided Finntraffic / Digitraffic Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 licence.

title: R Client for the Finntraffic Digitraffic API
text: MIT

what: digitraffic: R Client for the Finntraffic Digitraffic TMS (LAM) API — digitraffic-package
title: digitraffic: R Client for the Finntraffic Digitraffic TMS (LAM) API — digitraffic-package
text: Provides functions access traffic measurement data Finnish Transport Infrastructure Agency's Digitraffic open API. Covers TMS (Traffic Measurement System, known LAM Finnish) stations including real-time sensor data, station metadata, sensor calibration constants, historical raw vehicle-level observations. data returned tidy tibbles ready analysis.

title: digitraffic: R Client for the Finntraffic Digitraffic TMS (LAM) API — digitraffic-package
text: Maintainer: Theo Blauberg theo.blauberg@outlook.com (ORCID) [copyright holder] Authors: Theo Blauberg theo.blauberg@outlook.com (ORCID) [copyright holder]

what: Clear the in-session digitraffic cache — dt_cache_clear
title: Clear the in-session digitraffic cache — dt_cache_clear
text: Station metadata (dt_stations()) sensor metadata (dt_sensors()) cached 5 minutes within R session avoid redundant API calls. Call function force next call either function fetch fresh data API.

title: Clear the in-session digitraffic cache — dt_cache_clear
code: dt_cache_clear()

title: Clear the in-session digitraffic cache — dt_cache_clear
text: Invisibly returns NULL.

title: Clear the in-session digitraffic cache — dt_cache_clear
code: dt_cache_clear() #> digitraffic cache cleared.

what: Fetch raw historical vehicle-level data for a LAM/TMS station — dt_history_raw
title: Fetch raw historical vehicle-level data for a LAM/TMS station — dt_history_raw
text: Downloads parses raw CSV one station one day.  row represents single vehicle passage measurement loop. Raw data available December 2021 onwards published following day 08:00–09:00 EET.

title: Fetch raw historical vehicle-level data for a LAM/TMS station — dt_history_raw
code: dt_history_raw(tms_number, date)

title: Fetch raw historical vehicle-level data for a LAM/TMS station — dt_history_raw
text: tms_number single positive integer: station's tms_number (id).  Use dt_stations() read tms_number column. different id used dt_station_data(). date single Date object day retrieve, e.g. .Date("2024-03-15").  Must least one day past earlier 2021-12-01.

title: Fetch raw historical vehicle-level data for a LAM/TMS station — dt_history_raw
text: tibble one row per vehicle passage columns: station_id Integer. TMS station number file. year_short Integer. 2-digit year. day_of_year Integer. Day number (1–366). hour, minute, second, centisecond Integer. Passage time components. vehicle_length_m Double. Vehicle length metres. lane Integer. Lane number. direction Integer. 1 = increasing direction, 2 = decreasing. vehicle_class Integer. Class code 1–9 (see dt_vehicle_classes()). speed_kmh Integer. Speed km/h. quality_flag Integer. 0 = valid, 1 = outside valid range. interval_ms Integer. Time since previous vehicle (ms). time_in_loop_ms Integer. Time vehicle spent loop (ms). datetime POSIXct (Europe/Helsinki). Full passage timestamp. vehicle_class_label Character. English label vehicle class. vehicle_class_category Character. Broad category: "Car", "Truck", "Bus", "Motorcycle".

title: Fetch raw historical vehicle-level data for a LAM/TMS station — dt_history_raw
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # One day of data for station with tms_number = 1 raw <- dt_history_raw(tms_number = 1, date = as.Date("2024-03-15"))  # Hourly traffic volume by direction library(dplyr) raw |>   filter(quality_flag == 0) |>   mutate(hour_of_day = hour) |>   count(hour_of_day, direction) } # }

what: Get sensor calibration constants for LAM/TMS stations — dt_sensor_constants
title: Get sensor calibration constants for LAM/TMS stations — dt_sensor_constants
text: Fetches station-specific calibration values: free-flow speeds (VVAPAAS1/VVAPAAS2), maximum hourly capacity (MS1/MS2), road direction (Tien_suunta).  Many stations seasonal variants separate summer winter values.

title: Get sensor calibration constants for LAM/TMS stations — dt_sensor_constants
code: dt_sensor_constants(id = NULL)

title: Get sensor calibration constants for LAM/TMS stations — dt_sensor_constants
text: id NULL (default) return constants stations, single positive integer station id filter one station.

title: Get sensor calibration constants for LAM/TMS stations — dt_sensor_constants
text: tibble columns: station_id Integer. Station id. data_updated_time POSIXct (UTC). constants last updated. name Character. Constant name: "VVAPAAS1", "VVAPAAS2", "MS1", "MS2", "Tien_suunta". value Double. Constant value (speed km/h, count veh/h, bearing degrees). valid_from Character. Start validity period, "MM-DD" format. valid_to Character. End validity period, "MM-DD" format.

title: Get sensor calibration constants for LAM/TMS stations — dt_sensor_constants
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Constants for all stations dt_sensor_constants()  # Constants for a single station dt_sensor_constants(id = 23001)  # Free-flow speeds only library(dplyr) dt_sensor_constants() |>   filter(grepl("^VVAPAAS", name)) } # }

what: List all available LAM/TMS sensor types — dt_sensors
title: List all available LAM/TMS sensor types — dt_sensors
text: Fetches catalogue sensor types used across LAM stations. sensor unique id matches sensor_id column returned dt_station_data().

title: List all available LAM/TMS sensor types — dt_sensors
code: dt_sensors()

title: List all available LAM/TMS sensor types — dt_sensors
text: tibble columns: id Integer. Sensor identifier. name Character. Full Finnish sensor name, e.g. "OHITUKSET_5MIN_KIINTEA_SUUNTA1". short_name Character. Abbreviated label. unit Character. Measurement unit ("km/h", "kpl/h", "***"). direction Character. "INCREASING_DIRECTION", "DECREASING_DIRECTION", "UNKNOWN". description_fi Character. Finnish description. description_en Character. English description (often NA).

title: List all available LAM/TMS sensor types — dt_sensors
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ dt_sensors()  # Speed sensors only library(dplyr) dt_sensors() |> filter(unit == "km/h") } # }

what: Get detailed metadata for a single LAM/TMS station — dt_station
title: Get detailed metadata for a single LAM/TMS station — dt_station
text: Fetches extended metadata one station internal API id. look id first, use dt_stations().

title: Get detailed metadata for a single LAM/TMS station — dt_station
code: dt_station(id)

title: Get detailed metadata for a single LAM/TMS station — dt_station
text: id single positive integer: station's id returned dt_stations().  Note: tms_number (used historical CSV downloads via dt_history_raw()).

title: Get detailed metadata for a single LAM/TMS station — dt_station
text: one-row tibble columns: id, tms_number, name, name_fi, name_sv, name_en Identifiers multilingual names. longitude, latitude, elevation WGS-84 coordinates. bearing Road bearing degrees. collection_status, collection_interval, state Operational status. station_type Hardware type, e.g. "DSL_6". municipality, municipality_code Municipality name code. province, province_code Province name code. direction1_municipality, direction1_municipality_code Municipality direction 1. direction2_municipality, direction2_municipality_code Municipality direction 2. road_number, road_section, distance_from_section_start Road address. carriageway Carriageway descriptor. free_flow_speed1, free_flow_speed2 Free-flow speed (km/h) per direction. livi_id LIVI system identifier. purpose Measurement purpose. start_time, data_updated_time POSIXct (UTC) timestamps.

title: Get detailed metadata for a single LAM/TMS station — dt_station
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Look up a station id first stations <- dt_stations(name = "Espoo") dt_station(stations$id[[1]]) } # }

what: Get real-time sensor data for a single LAM/TMS station — dt_station_data
title: Get real-time sensor data for a single LAM/TMS station — dt_station_data
text: Fetches current sensor values one station.  Data updated approximately every minute.

title: Get real-time sensor data for a single LAM/TMS station — dt_station_data
code: dt_station_data(id)

title: Get real-time sensor data for a single LAM/TMS station — dt_station_data
text: id single positive integer: station's id (tms_number). Use dt_stations() look station ids.

title: Get real-time sensor data for a single LAM/TMS station — dt_station_data
text: tibble one row per sensor reading columns: station_id Integer. Station id. tms_number Integer. TMS system number. data_updated_time POSIXct (UTC). station data updated. sensor_id Integer. Sensor identifier (see dt_sensors()). name Character. Full Finnish sensor name. short_name Character. Abbreviated label. value Double. Measured value. unit Character. Unit, e.g. "km/h", "kpl/h", "***". measured_time POSIXct (UTC). measurement taken. time_window_start, time_window_end POSIXct (UTC). Aggregation window.

title: Get real-time sensor data for a single LAM/TMS station — dt_station_data
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Real-time speeds and counts for station 23001 dt_station_data(23001)  # Filter to speed sensors only library(dplyr) dt_station_data(23001) |> filter(unit == "km/h") } # }

what: List all LAM/TMS measurement stations — dt_stations
title: List all LAM/TMS measurement stations — dt_stations
text: Fetches metadata automatic traffic measurement (LAM/TMS) stations Digitraffic API.  Basic results cached 5 minutes per R session.  Extended columns (road_number, municipality, province) always included output: road_number parsed station name (extra API calls), municipality province joined bundled detailed cache (user-refreshed disk cache — see dt_stations_load_details()).

title: List all LAM/TMS measurement stations — dt_stations
code: dt_stations(   name = NULL,   road_number = NULL,   municipality = NULL,   province = NULL,   bbox = NULL )

title: List all LAM/TMS measurement stations — dt_stations
text: name NULL case-insensitive regular expression matched station names, e.g. "Espoo" "^vt1_". road_number NULL positive integer road number, e.g. 7 valtatie 7.  Parsed station name — fast, extra API calls. municipality NULL character string matched case-insensitively municipality column, e.g. "Espoo". Supports regex, e.g. "Helsinki|Espoo". province NULL character string matched case-insensitively province column, e.g. "Uusimaa".  Supports regex. bbox NULL length-4 numeric vector c(lon_min, lat_min, lon_max, lat_max) WGS-84 degrees.  Filters stations whose coordinates fall inside bounding box.

title: List all LAM/TMS measurement stations — dt_stations
text: tibble one row per matching station columns: id Integer. Internal API station identifier. tms_number Integer. TMS system number (used history CSV). name Character. Station name. road_number Integer. Road number parsed station name (e.g. 7 "vt7_Rita"). NA non-standard names. longitude Double. WGS-84 longitude. latitude Double. WGS-84 latitude. elevation Double. Elevation metres. bearing Integer. Road bearing degrees (0–360). municipality Character. Municipality name detailed cache. NA stations added cache last built. province Character. Province name detailed cache. NA stations added cache last built. collection_status Character. "GATHERING" "REMOVED_TEMPORARILY". state Character. Operational state, e.g. "OK". data_updated_time POSIXct (UTC). Time record last updated.

title: List all LAM/TMS measurement stations — dt_stations
text: live station list changed since cache built filter municipality province, warning emitted instructions refresh cache.

title: List all LAM/TMS measurement stations — dt_stations
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # All stations — road_number, municipality, province always present dt_stations()  # By name (regex supported) dt_stations(name = "Espoo") dt_stations(name = "^vt1_")  # By road number dt_stations(road_number = 7)  # By municipality (matches the municipality column you see in the output) dt_stations(municipality = "Espoo")  # By province dt_stations(province = "Uusimaa")  # By bounding box (Helsinki metropolitan area) dt_stations(bbox = c(24.5, 60.1, 25.2, 60.4))  # Combine filters dt_stations(road_number = 1, municipality = "Espoo") } # }

what: Get real-time sensor data for all LAM/TMS stations — dt_stations_data
title: Get real-time sensor data for all LAM/TMS stations — dt_stations_data
text: Fetches current sensor values every station single request. response typically several MB may take seconds. Data updated approximately every minute.

title: Get real-time sensor data for all LAM/TMS stations — dt_stations_data
code: dt_stations_data()

title: Get real-time sensor data for all LAM/TMS stations — dt_stations_data
text: tibble columns dt_station_data(), one row per sensor per station.

title: Get real-time sensor data for all LAM/TMS stations — dt_stations_data
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ all_data <- dt_stations_data()  # Average speed across all stations, direction 1 library(dplyr) all_data |>   filter(grepl("KESKINOPEUS_60MIN.*SUUNTA1", name)) |>   summarise(mean_speed = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE)) } # }

what: Fetch and cache detailed metadata for all LAM/TMS stations — dt_stations_load_details
title: Fetch and cache detailed metadata for all LAM/TMS stations — dt_stations_load_details
text: Downloads extended metadata (municipality, province, road number, …) every station using parallel HTTP requests saves result user cache directory (tools::R_user_dir("digitraffic", "cache")).

title: Fetch and cache detailed metadata for all LAM/TMS stations — dt_stations_load_details
code: dt_stations_load_details(max_active = 10L)

title: Fetch and cache detailed metadata for all LAM/TMS stations — dt_stations_load_details
text: max_active Integer. Maximum number concurrent HTTP requests. Default 10.  Increase faster fetching fast connection; decrease polite API.

title: Fetch and cache detailed metadata for all LAM/TMS stations — dt_stations_load_details
text: Invisibly returns detailed stations tibble.

title: Fetch and cache detailed metadata for all LAM/TMS stations — dt_stations_load_details
text: running function new filters dt_stations() — road_number, municipality, province, bbox — use refreshed data.  need re-run package warns cache date (.e., new stations added network).

title: Fetch and cache detailed metadata for all LAM/TMS stations — dt_stations_load_details
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ dt_stations_load_details() } # }

what: Vehicle classification codes used in LAM raw data — dt_vehicle_classes
title: Vehicle classification codes used in LAM raw data — dt_vehicle_classes
text: Returns lookup table mapping integer vehicle class codes (1-9) English Finnish labels, used vehicle_class column dt_history_raw().

title: Vehicle classification codes used in LAM raw data — dt_vehicle_classes
code: dt_vehicle_classes()

title: Vehicle classification codes used in LAM raw data — dt_vehicle_classes
text: tibble columns: vehicle_class Integer. Class code (1-9). label_en Character. English label. label_fi Character. Finnish label (UTF-8). category_en Character. Broad vehicle category English: "Car" (classes 1, 6, 7), "Truck" (classes 2, 4, 5, 9), "Bus" (class 3), "Motorcycle" (class 8). category_fi Character. Broad vehicle category Finnish (UTF-8).

title: Vehicle classification codes used in LAM raw data — dt_vehicle_classes
code: dt_vehicle_classes() #> # A tibble: 9 × 5 #>   vehicle_class label_en                  label_fi       category_en category_fi #>           <int> <chr>                     <chr>          <chr>       <chr>       #> 1             1 Car / van                 Henkilö- tai … Car         Henkilöauto #> 2             2 Truck (no trailer)        Kuorma-auto i… Truck       Kuorma-auto #> 3             3 Bus                       Linja-auto     Bus         Linja-auto  #> 4             4 Truck + semitrailer       Kuorma-auto j… Truck       Kuorma-auto #> 5             5 Truck + full trailer      Kuorma-auto j… Truck       Kuorma-auto #> 6             6 Car + trailer             Henkilöauto j… Car         Henkilöauto #> 7             7 Car + caravan / motorhome Henkilöauto j… Car         Henkilöauto #> 8             8 Motorcycle / moped        Moottoripyörä… Motorcycle  Moottoripy… #> 9             9 High Capacity Truck       HCT-ajoneuvoy… Truck       Kuorma-auto
